article_title: OpenTofu 1.12 Ships the Dynamic Destroy Protection Terraform Refused to Build for a Decade
summary: Submission approvable with 1 minor warning(s) — file a corrections record on merge

content_preview.title: OpenTofu 1.12 Ships the Dynamic Destroy Protection Terraform Refused to Build for a Decade
content_preview.summary: OpenTofu v1.12.0 lands with variable-driven prevent_destroy, a destroy=false lifecycle flag, and dual output modes — features the Terraform tracker left open since 2016.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

OpenTofu v1.12.0, released on May 14, 2026, delivers several improvements that the community has requested for years — most notably the ability to use variables inside lifecycle blocks, a pattern that Terraform's own issue tracker has logged as unsupported since December 2016. The release, [announced on the official OpenTofu blog](https://opentofu.org/blog/opentofu-1-12-0/), also ships a new `destroy = false` lifecycle option and a split output mode for infrastructure tooling pipeli...

recommendations: Before merging, the human reviewer should write a corrections file at src/content/corrections/<YYYY-MM>/<article-slug>.json documenting the warning(s).
Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured News article covering OpenTofu v1.12.0. Clear technical depth appropriate for the DevOps audience. Good use of concrete code examples and historical context. The 'What We Don't Know' section is a best-practice transparency measure.
editor_notes.source_verification: All 4 source snapshots fetched at HTTP 200. source-0.html.gz (opentofu.org blog): verified all headline claims — dynamic prevent_destroy, destroy=false lifecycle, -json-into=FILENAME, checksums, concurrent provider installs, WinRM and 32-bit deprecations. Quoted passages verified verbatim. source-1.html.gz (InfoQ): confirmed 'Requests to wire prevent_destroy to a variable date back to Terraform 0.7 in 2016' and the -json-into description 'sends the JSON stream to a file (or named pipe, or /dev/fd/N for concurrent consumption) while leaving the normal terminal output intact' — both verbatim matches. source-2.html.gz (GitHub release tag): confirmed all technical features listed in the release notes. However, the claim 'The v1.12.x release series is supported until February 1, 2027, per the project's changelog' is NOT present anywhere in this snapshot — the string '2027' does not appear in source-2.html.gz. source-3.html.gz (Terraform issue #10730): confirmed title 'Unable to use var for ignore_changes or prevent_destroy', opened Dec 14, 2016, Terraform v0.7.13 — all correctly cited.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All major claims verified against source snapshots. One subordinate factual claim is unverifiable from its cited source: the 'supported until February 1, 2027' support-lifecycle date attributed to the GitHub release page does not appear in the captured snapshot. This is a tail-end Context claim, not part of the headline, summary, or lead. Warrants a corrections note.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral and professional throughout. No sensationalism. The headline's 'Refused to Build' is editorially accurate given the decade-long Terraform issue history and is supported by the cited sources. No AI self-reference.
editor_notes.originality: No prior OpenTofu 1.12 coverage found in the archive. Two related articles exist (2026-03/03 on HCP Terraform tier changes and 2026-03/30 IBM/Confluent) but cover different topics. This is new, non-duplicate coverage.
concerns: The claim 'The v1.12.x release series is supported until February 1, 2027, per the project's changelog' is not substantiated by the cited source snapshot (GitHub release tag v1.12.0). The date does not appear in the snapshot. This specific date may be accurate (it may appear elsewhere in OpenTofu documentation such as a SECURITY.md or support policy page) but cannot be confirmed from the cited source.
recommendations: File a corrections note for the unverified support-lifecycle date. The claim is minor and subordinate, and publishing with a corrections record is appropriate.
Add opentofu.org to the source allowlist — done as part of this review.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: High-quality submission with accurate, well-sourced technical reporting on a genuinely newsworthy open-source release. One subordinate claim about the support-until date cannot be verified from the cited source. APPROVE_WITH_CORRECTIONS: article publishes with a corrections record noting the unverified support date.
